title: Spike — Validate iCloud Sync Behavior and Transcription API
creator: [You]

userStory.persona: a developer setting up Utterd's audio pipeline
userStory.want: confirm how iCloud Voice Memos sync works and whether the macOS 26 transcription API meets our needs in a daemon context
userStory.soThat: the remaining stories are built on verified assumptions and we can identify blocking risks before investing in production code

questionsToAnswer: How do new Voice Memo files appear in the sync directory? Are there placeholder files, partial downloads, or atomic writes?
What is the reliable method to detect that a file is fully synced and ready for processing?
What is the exact file path pattern for the Voice Memos sync directory on macOS 26?
Can SFSpeechRecognizer process .m4a files on-device without format conversion?
Is there a maximum audio duration limit for on-device recognition?
How long does transcription take for a 1-minute memo? A 5-minute memo?
Does on-device recognition function in a background daemon process with no foreground app?
Can speech recognition authorization be requested and persisted by a LaunchAgent daemon?
definitionOfDone: Every question above has a documented answer with evidence (sample code output, screenshots, or logs)
Any assumption from the architecture document that was invalidated is flagged and an alternative approach is proposed
Findings are written into Section 2 of the architecture document
If any finding is a blocker (transcription doesn't work in a daemon context, .m4a isn't supported), the team is notified immediately — don't wait for the timebox to end
note: The output is knowledge, not production code. All code written during the spike is throwaway.
